model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the button in the top-left corner
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=-308 dy=-165 conf=0.90 -> (53,40)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click a button in the top-left corner.
The target element is the green button at the top left.
The current crosshair (red) is located inside a blue circle.
The blue circle is centered at (53, 40) approximately, based on the text at the bottom right.
The crosshair is at a certain position. Let's estimate its position relative to the target button center.
The target button is the green rectangle.
Its center is approximately at (53, 40) according to the previous iteration's result and the bottom right text.
The red crosshair is inside the blue circle.
Let's find the center of the red crosshair.
The blue circle center seems to be around (70, 50) in the current image coordinate system if I look at the image.
Wait, the text says (53, 40) +/- 30px.
Let's look at the green button. Its bounds are roughly [10, 10, 95, 70]. Center is approx (52, 40).
The red crosshair is at approximately (70, 55).
Offset from crosshair to target center:
dx = 53 - 70 = -17
dy = 40 - 55 = -15
Let's refine this.
The red crosshair is at roughly x=70, y=55.
Target center is around x=53, y=40.
dx = 53 - 70 = -17
dy = 40 - 55 = -15
Confidence: the crosshair is close but not on it. Let's say 0.8.
